scenario_title: Delete RRset and add RR of same type in one UPDATE

group_1.behavior: Applies the delete_rrset and add_rr operations within the same UPDATE as a replace-like transaction.

group_2.behavior: Treats the mixed delete_rrset/add_rr sequence for the same owner/type as conflicting or otherwise non-committable, so the update is rejected or not applied.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4 and §3.4.2 define delete_rrset and add_rr, but do not specify the required semantics when both target the same owner/type in one UPDATE message. RFC 2181 §5.4 discusses RRSet formation, not transaction ordering within Dynamic Update. Therefore implementations may differ on whether the message is processed as an in-message replacement, rejected as conflicting, or left unapplied, producing observable differences in both UPDATE reply and follow-up QUERY.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: This test isolates the intra-message ordering ambiguity by starting with a single A RRset and issuing one UPDATE that first deletes that RRset and then adds a new A RR at the same name. Because the protocol does not define whether those operations compose as a replace, are conflicting, or must be processed strictly in order, servers can diverge either in the UPDATE RCODE or in the final RRset contents. A single follow-up QUERY makes the outcome binary and directly observable.
